vendor_context: Priority Bicycles competitor (eBike / value commuter segment)
status: ACTIVE — High-growth DTC eBike brand; US market dominant, now expanding into Canada

networks.name: Impact
networks.notes: Primary network. Program managed via Impact platform. Application via lectricebikes.com. Content publishers, cycling sites, review channels eligible. Moderate barrier to entry — new/low-traffic sites may be rejected.

company_status.financial: STABLE — Private, Arizona-based; no distress signals
company_status.positioning: Value-leader in US eBike market; high-volume, affordable folding and commuter eBikes

expansion.notes: Canada market entry announced April 2, 2026. Partnership centered on infrastructure advocacy and cycling access. First international expansion move. Canadian affiliate program status: UNKNOWN — likely to follow via Impact, but not yet confirmed.

competitive_intel.commission_vs_category: Lectric 3% < Aventon 4% < Rad ~6% — Lectric has lowest published rate, but high AOV ($928) yields ~$27.85 avg payout per sale
competitive_intel.affiliate_risk: LOW — Stable brand, consistent Impact program, strong consumer recognition
competitive_intel.canada_affiliate_opportunity: WATCH — If Lectric launches a Canadian affiliate tier on Impact, it opens new geo-targeted traffic opportunities. No confirmed CA program yet.
competitive_intel.opportunity_for_priority: Priority Bicycles competes for same cycling content affiliates (review sites, cycling YouTubers). Priority's edge: zero maintenance, no battery anxiety, lower price point. Lectric's saturation in value eBike space may push affiliates to seek differentiated stories — Priority fits that niche.

history.event: First audit. No prior file. Confirmed Impact as primary network, 3% commission, 30-day cookie, ~$928 AOV, ~$0.97 EPC. Notable: Canada expansion announced April 2, 2026 via Vélo Canada Bikes partnership. 650k+ units sold in 6 years. Canadian affiliate program not yet confirmed.
